source.description: Six-panel contact sheet generated with the built-in image tool, reviewed before cropping, then cropped with scripts/crop-contact-sheet.py.
source.notes: Chosen as the June 10 replacement after the flag lesson was held for a July 4 release.

process_strategy: A single contact sheet keeps the glass, straw, lemon slice, ice cubes, liquid line, and cast shadow in the same position from construction through finish.

final_elements.name: tall glass outline
final_elements.description: A slightly tapered drinking glass with elliptical rim and base.

final_elements.name: lemonade liquid level
final_elements.description: A curved horizontal liquid line inside the transparent glass.

final_elements.name: bendy straw
final_elements.description: A diagonal straw rising out of the glass with a small bend near the top.

final_elements.name: lemon wheel garnish
final_elements.description: A round lemon slice clipped to the rim with wedge segments.

final_elements.name: ice cubes
final_elements.description: A few small tilted cubes visible through the lemonade.

final_elements.name: condensation dots
final_elements.description: Tiny droplets and short marks on the outside of the glass.

final_elements.name: table shadow
final_elements.description: A soft oval graphite shadow beneath the glass.

final_elements.name: yellow lemonade color
final_elements.description: Restrained yellow colored-pencil fill inside the glass and lemon slice.

frames.visible_job: Draws the rim ellipse, tapered glass sides, base ellipse, and center guide.
Adds the curved liquid line and lower glass thickness while preserving the transparent form.
Adds the diagonal bendy straw rising from inside the glass.
Adds the lemon wheel garnish, wedge marks, and visible ice cubes.
Adds condensation marks, table shadow, and light yellow colored-pencil fill.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies transparent overlaps, and strengthens existing yellow shading.
allowed_changes: minor cleanup of already introduced lemon, ice, straw, and droplet details

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
